2605.21308.title: Deformba: Vision State Space Model with Adaptive State Fusion
2605.21308.paper_summary: State Space Models (SSMs) have emerged as a powerful and efficient alternative to Transformers, demonstrating linear-time complexity and exceptional sequence modeling capabilities. However, their application to vision tasks remains challenging. First, existing vision SSMs largely depend on manually designed fixed scanning methods to flatten image patches into sequences, which imposes predefined geometric structures and increases the complexity. Second, the broader adoption of vision SSMs is hindered in domains that require query-based interactions between distinct information streams. This is a result of the inherently causal and self-referential nature of SSMs designed for 1D sequence modeling tasks. This fusion mechanism is indispensable for critical perception tasks such as multi-view 3D fusion. To address these limitations, we propose Deformba, a context adaptive method that dynamically augments the spatial structural information while maintaining the linear complexity of SSMs. Deformba also allows multi-modal fusion like cross attention. To demonstrate the effectiveness and general applicability of Deformba, we test its performance on general 2D vision tasks such as image classification, object detection, and segmentation, as well as 3D vision tasks like BEV perception. Extensive experiments show that Deformba achieves strong performance across various visual perception benchmarks.
2605.21308.paper_summary_zh: 狀態空間模型（SSMs）已成為一種強大且高效的替代方案，展現出線性時間複雜度和卓越的序列建模能力。<lb/>然而，它們在視覺任務中的應用仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>首先，現有的視覺SSMs在很大程度上依賴手動設計的固定掃描方法將圖像補丁展平為序列，這會施加預定義的幾何結構並增加複雜性。<lb/>其次，視覺SSMs的廣泛應用受到需要不同信息流之間基於查詢的交互的領域的阻礙。<lb/>這是由於為1D序列建模任務設計的SSMs固有的因果性和自我參照性質所致。<lb/>這種融合機制對於多視角3D融合等關鍵感知任務是不可或缺的。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們提出了Deformba，一種上下文自適應方法，能夠在保持SSMs線性複雜度的同時動態增強空間結構信息。<lb/>Deformba還允許像交叉注意力這樣的多模態融合。<lb/>為了展示Deformba的有效性和普遍適用性，我們在一般的2D視覺任務上進行了性能測試，例如圖像分類、物體檢測和分割，以及像BEV感知這樣的3D視覺任務。<lb/>大量實驗表明，Deformba在各種視覺感知基準上都取得了強勁的表現。
2605.21308.author: Hongyu Ke et.al.
2605.21308.authors: Hongyu Ke, Jack Morris, Yongkang Liu, Satoshi Kitai, Kentaro Oguchi, Yi Ding, Haoxin Wang
